heroPoems.title: I had thousands of desires, each fierce enough to take my life.
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I had thousands of desires, each fierce enough to take my life.
Many were fulfilled, yet they still felt too few.
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: The Way through the Woods
heroPoems.firstStanza:
They shut the road through the woods
Seventy years ago.
Weather and rain have undone it again,
And now you would never know
There was once a road through the woods
Before they planted the trees.
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: Cousin Nancy
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Miss Nancy Ellicott Strode across the hills and broke them,
Rode across the hills and broke them--
The barren New England hills--
Riding to hounds
Over the cow-pasture.

heroPoems.title: I felt a funeral in my brain
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I felt a funeral in my brain,
And mourners, to and fro,
Kept treading, treading, till it seemed
That sense was breaking through.
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

heroPoems.title: Sword for Uncertainty
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Cut doubt with clear seeing—
then move.
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

heroPoems.title: Fancy
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Ever let the Fancy roam,
Pleasure never is at home:
At a touch sweet Pleasure melteth,
Like to bubbles when rain pelteth;
Then let winged Fancy wander
Through the thought still spread beyond her:

heroPoems.title: Oread
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Whirl up, sea—
whirl your pointed pines,
splash your great pines
on our rocks,
hurl your green over us,
cover us with your pools of fir.

heroPoems.title: No Better Thing
heroPoems.firstStanza:
There is nothing better
than to eat,
to drink,
and to find good in your labor.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: I must launch out my boat. The languid
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I must launch out my boat. The languid hours pass by on the shore—Alas
for me!
The spring has done its flowering and taken leave. And now with the
burden of faded futile flowers I wait and linger.
The waves have become clamorous, and upon the bank in the shady lane
the yellow leaves flutter and fall.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: Evening in a Sugar Orchard
heroPoems.firstStanza:
From where I lingered in a lull in March
Outside the sugar-house one night for choice,
I called the fireman with a careful voice
And bade him leave the pan and stoke the arch:
“O fireman, give the fire another stoke,
And send more sparks up chimney with the smoke.”
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

collections.name: The Sound of Returning
collections.tagline: For when a chair remembers you.
collections.theme: Poems that hold small domestic scenes until they become almost sacred.

featuredPoem.title: Many Plans
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Many plans live
in a human heart,

poems.title: The same stream of life that runs through
The garden bird warned the rose at dawn
In a Station of the Metro

collections.name: Before the Door Opens
collections.tagline: For the warmth after cold air.
collections.theme: Language for the suspended hour between conversation and sleep.

featuredPoem.title: Tell me, O Swan, your ancient tale.
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Tell me, O Swan, your ancient tale.
From what land do you come, O Swan? to what shore will you fly?

poems.title: If I can stop one heart from breaking
To the Virgins, to Make Much of Time

collections.name: A Small Province of Night
collections.tagline: For when breath slows at last.
collections.theme: A gathering of poems where memory and present tense overlap gently.

featuredPoem.title: At the beginning of love, why cry as if this is the end?
featuredPoem.firstLines:
At the beginning of love, why cry as if this is the end?
There is much more to happen.

poems.title: Everything hammered in the forge of life turns to nothing
